description: Multi-surface Phase 6: startSurface's BROWSER descriptor opens a blank ready session explicitly (the goTo-opener sibling). Covers the named form with launch overrides (headless + size), the engine-only form (name defaults to the engine keyword), viewport (which wins over size for page dimensions), activation (a just-opened surface is the active one for surface-less steps), goTo navigating the opened session by name, and whole-browser closeSurface. Runs in the default headless-firefox context on every platform; engine-only descriptors use chrome so the default firefox session's name is never duplicated (an unnamed same-engine descriptor is a documented duplicate-name FAIL, covered at unit level).

tests.description: Open a second, named firefox session with explicit headless + size overrides; the new session is active (surface-less goTo navigates it), by-name addressing works, and closeSurface ends it with focus falling back to the default session.

steps.description: The default session exists first, with a page loaded
startSurface opens a SECOND firefox session (blank, ready)
The opened session is the active surface: a surface-less goTo navigates IT, not the default session
The default session still shows its own page (by engine keyword)
Address the opened session by name
Close the opened session by name; focus falls back to the default

tests.description: Engine-only descriptor: the name defaults to the engine keyword (chrome — distinct from the default firefox session), and `viewport` sets exact page dimensions post-start (runBrowserScript reads window.innerWidth back).

steps.description: goTo selects the opened chrome session by engine keyword (no duplicate open)
The viewport override landed: the page sees exactly 800 CSS pixels
